quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Carbon fixation"?

options.text: A series of molecular events by which a signal on a cell's surface is converted to a specific cellular response
A simple sugar (glucose, fructose, galactose) with the general formula (CH₂O)n; the monomer of carbohydrates
A gene encoding a protein that inhibits cell division (e.g., p53 triggers apoptosis in damaged cells, Rb blocks G1/S transition)
The incorporation of CO₂ into an organic molecule (RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA); catalyzed by RuBisCO

quiz_questions.explanation: "Carbon fixation" is defined as: The incorporation of CO₂ into an organic molecule (RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA); catalyzed by RuBisCO. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "RuBisCO"?

options.text: A series of protein kinases that sequentially phosphorylate and activate each other, amplifying the signal at each step
The totality of an organism's chemical reactions; catabolism (breakdown, releases energy) and anabolism (synthesis, requires energy)
Double-membrane organelle where cellular respiration produces ATP; has own circular DNA, supporting the endosymbiotic theory
Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration)

quiz_questions.explanation: "RuBisCO" is defined as: Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "G3P (glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate)"?

options.text: Differential survival and reproduction of individuals with favorable heritable traits in a given environment; the primary mechanism of adaptive evolution
Exchange of genetic material between homologous chromosomes during prophase I of meiosis; increases genetic variation
A gene-editing system using guide RNA to direct the Cas9 nuclease to cut DNA at a precise location, enabling precise gene knockout, correction, or insertion
A 3-carbon sugar produced by the Calvin cycle; the direct product used to build glucose, amino acids, and fatty acids

quiz_questions.explanation: "G3P (glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate)" is defined as: A 3-carbon sugar produced by the Calvin cycle; the direct product used to build glucose, amino acids, and fatty acids. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Regeneration of RuBP"?

options.text: A measure of disorder or randomness; the 2nd law of thermodynamics states that entropy of the universe increases in spontaneous processes
The phase of the Calvin cycle using 3 ATP to regenerate the 5-carbon RuBP acceptor from remaining G3P molecules
The proportion of a specific allele in a population: p = frequency of dominant allele, q = frequency of recessive allele; p + q = 1
The energy available to do work; ΔG  0 (endergonic, requires energy input), ΔG = 0 (equilibrium)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Regeneration of RuBP" is defined as: The phase of the Calvin cycle using 3 ATP to regenerate the 5-carbon RuBP acceptor from remaining G3P molecules. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Photorespiration"?

options.text: RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures
Germline mutations (in eggs/sperm) are passed to offspring; somatic mutations affect only the individual and are not inherited
The rate at which producers store energy as biomass minus their own respiratory losses; NPP = GPP - respiration
Speciation occurring within the same geographic area, often through polyploidy (plants) or habitat/behavioral specialization

quiz_questions.explanation: "Photorespiration" is defined as: RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The incorporation of CO₂ into an organic molecule (RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA); catalyzed by RuBisCO"?

options.text: Pre-mRNA processing
Species richness
Carbon fixation
Hydrogen bond

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Carbon fixation".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration)"?

options.text: Chitin
RuBisCO
Semiconservative replication
Electron transport chain

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "RuBisCO".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A 3-carbon sugar produced by the Calvin cycle; the direct product used to build glucose, amino acids, and fatty acids"?

options.text: r-selected species
Alternative splicing
G3P (glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate)
Law of Segregation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "G3P (glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The phase of the Calvin cycle using 3 ATP to regenerate the 5-carbon RuBP acceptor from remaining G3P molecules"?

options.text: Ecosystem services
Genotype frequency
Regeneration of RuBP
Denaturation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Regeneration of RuBP".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures"?

options.text: Recombination frequency
Molecular clock
Photorespiration
Biodiversity

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Photorespiration".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle is accurate?

options.text: Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle occurs in the chloroplast stroma and fixes atmospheric CO₂ into organic molecules using ATP and NADPH from the light reactions.
Photosystems II and I absorb photons, exciting electrons that pass through electron transport chains.
Enzymes are typically proteins with a specific active site that binds substrates via the induced fit model.
Water is split (photolysis) at PSII, releasing O₂.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: Biotechnology Applications encompasses the tools and techniques used to manipulate DNA.
Sexual selection and artificial selection are special cases of selection that shape traits beyond survival.
The enzyme RuBisCO catalyzes carbon fixation (CO₂ + RuBP → 2 molecules of 3-PGA), making it the most abundant protein on Earth.
Natural selection acts on phenotypic variation, favoring traits that increase fitness (reproductive success) in a specific environment.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: Eukaryotic cells compartmentalize functions in membrane-bound organelles: the nucleus (DNA storage), endoplasmic reticulum (protein/lipid synthesis), Golgi apparatus (modification and sorting), mitochondria (ATP production), and chloroplasts...
Water is split (photolysis) at PSII, releasing O₂.
While most mutations are neutral or harmful, some confer advantages (e.g., sickle cell heterozygote advantage against malaria).
Three turns of the Calvin cycle fix 3 CO₂ to produce one net G3P molecule, consuming 9 ATP and 6 NADPH.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: DNA Replication and Transcription describes how genetic information stored in DNA is copied (replication) and read into mRNA (transcription).
Protein denaturation (loss of 3D shape from heat, pH, or chemical changes) destroys function, demonstrating that structure determines function—a central theme in AP Biology.
G3P is used to synthesize glucose and other organic molecules.
Alternative splicing enables one gene to produce multiple protein variants.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: Helicase unwinds the double helix, primase synthesizes RNA primers, and DNA polymerase III synthesizes new strands in the 5'→3' direction.

The fossil record shows transitional forms (Tiktaalik between fish and tetrapods), comparative anatomy reveals homologous structures (bat wing, whale flipper, human arm sharing the same bone pattern), molecular biology shows DNA/protein sequence...
Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle occurs in the chloroplast stroma and fixes atmospheric CO₂ into organic molecules using ATP and NADPH from the light reactions.
Nutrients (carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, water) cycle between biotic and abiotic components.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: C4 and CAM photosynthesis as evolutionary adaptations to minimize photorespiration in hot, dry environments (corn, sugarcane, cacti, pineapple)
Drug design targeting specific enzyme active sites: HIV protease inhibitors blocking viral protein processing to treat AIDS
Forensic genetics using Mendelian inheritance patterns to establish paternity or identify family relationships from DNA evidence
CRISPR-Cas9 clinical trials treating sickle cell disease by editing the BCL11A gene in patient stem cells to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: Rising atmospheric CO₂ levels increasing carbon fixation rates in some plants (CO₂ fertilization effect) but potentially decreasing nutritional quality of crops
Cancer epigenetics: abnormal DNA methylation patterns silencing tumor suppressor genes or activating oncogenes without DNA mutations
Telomere shortening with each cell division limiting cell lifespan; telomerase activation in cancer cells enables unlimited replication (immortality)

Wolf reintroduction into Yellowstone (1995) demonstrating trophic cascades: wolves reduced elk overgrazing, allowing riparian vegetation recovery, which stabilized stream banks and increased beaver populations

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Caloric content of foods measured by the energy released during complete oxidation: carbohydrates ~4 kcal/g, lipids ~9 kcal/g, proteins ~4 kcal/g
Golden Rice engineered with β-carotene biosynthesis genes to combat vitamin A deficiency in developing countries, preventing blindness in millions of children
Biofuel production engineering algae with enhanced Calvin cycle efficiency to convert CO₂ into lipids for biodiesel
Artificial selection in agriculture: selective breeding transforming teosinte (small, hard kernels) into modern corn over thousands of years of human-directed selection

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle?

options.text: C4 and CAM photosynthesis as evolutionary adaptations to minimize photorespiration in hot, dry environments (corn, sugarcane, cacti, pineapple)
Telomere shortening with each cell division limiting cell lifespan; telomerase activation in cancer cells enables unlimited replication (immortality)
Mass extinctions (e.g., K-Pg, 66 MYA) eliminating 75% of species but opening ecological niches that enabled mammalian diversification and eventual human evolution
Cancer epigenetics: abnormal DNA methylation patterns silencing tumor suppressor genes or activating oncogenes without DNA mutations

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle in practice?

options.text: Rising atmospheric CO₂ levels increasing carbon fixation rates in some plants (CO₂ fertilization effect) but potentially decreasing nutritional quality of crops
Stem cell differentiation controlled by sequential gene regulation, with the same genome producing >200 different specialized cell types in the human body
Virus tracking during COVID-19 pandemic using SARS-CoV-2 genome sequencing to build phylogenetic trees tracing transmission chains and identifying new variants
The Human Genome Project using genetic and physical mapping to determine the positions of all ~20,000 human genes across 23 chromosome pairs

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Photosynthesis: Calvin Cycle.
